2605.02814.title: IConFace: Identity-Structure Asymmetric Conditioning for Unified Reference-Aware Face Restoration
2605.02814.paper_summary: Blind face restoration is highly ill-posed under severe degradation, where identity-critical details may be missing from the degraded input. Same-identity references reduce this ambiguity, but mismatched pose, expression, illumination, age, makeup, or local facial states can lead to overuse of reference appearance. We propose \textbf{IConFace}, a unified reference-aware and no-reference framework with identity--structure asymmetric conditioning. References are distilled into a norm-weighted global AdaFace identity anchor for image-only modulation, while the degraded image is reinforced as the spatial structure anchor through low-rank residuals and block-wise degraded cross-attention with two-route memory. The resulting single checkpoint exploits references when available and falls back to no-reference restoration when absent, improving identity consistency, fine-detail recovery, and degraded-only restoration quality in a unified model.
2605.02814.paper_summary_zh: 盲臉恢復在嚴重退化的情況下是高度不適定的，其中身份關鍵細節可能在退化的輸入中缺失。相同身份的參考可以減少這種模糊性，但姿勢、表情、光照、年齡、化妝或局部面部狀態的不匹配可能導致過度使用參考外觀。我們提出了\textbf{IConFace}，這是一個統一的參考感知和無參考框架，具有身份-結構不對稱條件。參考被提煉為一個標準加權的全局AdaFace身份錨點，用於僅圖像的調製，而退化的圖像則通過低秩殘差和基於區塊的退化交叉注意力與雙路記憶強化為空間結構錨點。所得到的單一檢查點在可用時利用參考，並在缺失時回退到無參考恢復，從而在統一模型中改善身份一致性、細節恢復和僅退化恢復質量。
2605.02814.author: Axi Niu et.al.
2605.02814.authors: Axi Niu, Jinyang Zhang, Senyan Qing
